---
title: 'catalog default'
---

Show or change the persisted **default catalog name** — used when subsequent
`xorq catalog` invocations don't supply `-n/--name` or `-p/--path`.

## Usage

```bash
xorq catalog default              # show current default
xorq catalog default --set <name> # persist a new default
xorq catalog default --unset      # revert to built-in default ("default")
```

## Options

| Option | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `--set NAME` | none | Persist `NAME` as the default catalog. |
| `--unset` | off | Remove the persisted default (reverts to `default`). |

`--set` and `--unset` are mutually exclusive.

## Resolution order

The active default is resolved in this order:

1. `XORQ_DEFAULT_CATALOG` environment variable (highest precedence).
2. The persisted config file at `~/.config/xorq/catalog-default` (set via `--set`).
3. The built-in default name (`default`).

The bare `xorq catalog default` command prints the resolved name and the source
that supplied it.

## Examples

```bash
# Show current default and where it came from
$ xorq catalog default
analytics  (source: config (~/.config/xorq/catalog-default))

# Change persisted default
xorq catalog default --set analytics

# Remove persisted default
xorq catalog default --unset
```
